 {"id":"asupersync-xrc.3.1","title":"Extend lab runtime to model N workers deterministically","description":"# Deterministic N-Worker Lab Runtime Model

## Purpose
Phase 0 lab runtime is single-threaded. Phase 1 needs a deterministic model of multiple workers.

## Plan-of-Record
- Represent the runtime as a set of worker states.
- Each “step” chooses:
  - which worker runs next
  - which task that worker polls (respecting lane priorities)
- Choice is controlled by:
  - explicit seed
  - optional externally-provided schedule (Phase 5)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Same seed/config produces identical traces.
- A captured schedule can be replayed.

## Testing
- Run identical parallel scenarios twice and compare traces.
- Stress: many tasks with steals; determinism must still hold.

 {"id":"asupersync-xrc.4","title":"Phase 1: Parallel Verification Suite","description":"# Phase 1: Parallel Verification Suite

## Purpose
Extend Phase 0’s verification (oracles, unit tests, E2E scenarios, benchmarks) to cover the parallel scheduler and region heap.

## Required Additions
- Stress tests for work-stealing correctness
- Determinism tests for parallel lab simulation
- Region heap safety tests
- Performance baselines for parallel spawn/scheduling overhead

## Acceptance Criteria
- All Phase 0 tests still pass.
- New parallel tests cover:
  - no duplicate polls
  - no lost tasks
  - cancellation drains under contention
  - region close quiescence under parallel scheduling

 {"id":"asupersync-xrc.4.1","title":"Add parallel stress tests (work stealing, cancellation, quiescence)","description":"# Parallel Stress Tests

## Purpose
Validate Phase 1 under load:
- work-stealing correctness
- cancellation drain under contention
- region close quiescence with migrated tasks

## Scenarios
- Many short tasks; ensure all complete (no duplicates/no loss).
- Many long tasks; cancel parent region; ensure drain completes and tasks terminal.
- Mix timers + steals.

## Logging / Debuggability
On failure, dump:
- trace
- per-worker queue snapshots
- first invariant violation evidence

## Acceptance Criteria
- Tests pass deterministically under lab simulation.
- Failures are reproducible via seed and/or saved schedule.

Existing lab quiescence determinism tests cover region close quiescence. -{"id":"asupersync-xrc.5","title":"Phase 1+: plan DAG builder + lawful rewrites","description":"# Phase 1+: plan DAG builder + lawful rewrites

## Purpose
The design includes an optional but high-leverage `plan` module:
- build a DAG of concurrency combinators
- apply lawful rewrites (based on semiring laws and observational equivalence)
- dedupe shared work (e.g., `race(join(a,b), join(a,c)) ≃ join(a, race(b,c))`)

This is both a performance feature and a correctness feature (by making rewrites semantics-preserving).

## Requirements
- Define a DAG IR for computations.
- Encode rewrites that are valid under the chosen policies.
- Provide tooling to explain rewrites (for debugging).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Demonstrate at least one dedup rewrite.
- Provide tests that validate equivalence under lab runtime.

 {"id":"asupersync-xrc.5.1","title":"Define plan IR for join/race/timeout DAGs","description":"# plan IR (DAG Representation)

## Purpose
Define a representation of concurrent computations suitable for optimization:
- nodes represent primitive/derived combinators
- edges represent data/control dependencies

## Requirements
- Preserve semantic identity (task/region boundaries) so rewrites remain valid.
- Avoid encoding “implementation scheduling details” into the IR.

## Acceptance Criteria
- A minimal IR can represent:
  - join
  - race
  - timeout
  - simple pipelines

 {"id":"asupersync-xrc.5.2","title":"Implement lawful rewrite engine (policy-aware)","description":"# Lawful Rewrite Engine (Policy-Aware)

## Purpose
Apply semiring-style rewrite rules safely:
- rewrites must be valid under the chosen policy and observational equivalence

## Candidate Rewrite (from spec)
`race(join(a,b), join(a,c)) ≃ join(a, race(b,c))` (dedupe shared `a`)

## Requirements
- Only apply rewrites when their preconditions hold:
  - independence assumptions
  - policy commutativity/associativity conditions

## Acceptance Criteria
- At least one rewrite implemented with clear preconditions.
- Tests show the rewritten plan produces equivalent outcomes and preserves invariants.

 {"id":"asupersync-xrc.5.3","title":"Add equivalence tests for plan rewrites (lab runtime oracle-driven)","description":"# plan Rewrite Equivalence Tests

## Purpose
Prove rewrites preserve semantics using the lab runtime as the executable semantics.

## Approach
- For each rewrite rule:
  - generate (or hand-construct) small programs where preconditions hold
  - run original and rewritten plans under the same lab config
  - compare:
    - final outcomes
    - invariant oracles
    - (optionally) canonicalized traces

## Acceptance Criteria
- Rewrite tests are deterministic and reproducible.
